extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won his third sprint stage after Quick-Step controlled the entire day, reeling in breakaway inside final 7km in Seville.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step initiated chase 40km out despite having already won both sprint stages, revealing aggressive accumulation strategy.
Breakaway reduced Dimension Data to duo before capture, suggesting team sacrificed numbers early rather than defending numerical advantage.
Trentin winning all three available sprint stages indicates rivals unable or unwilling to commit full leadout trains.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: described as having 'more relaxed stage' after previous day's troubles, maintaining 59-second GC lead
rider_signals.FRYDAY Omar: negative: abandoned race despite winning last two Vuelta mountain classifications, suggesting form collapse mid-race
